Last updated February 25, 2026.

Become a Dietitian in Minnesota

To apply for a Dietitian or Nutritionist license in Minnesota, you must use the Minnesota Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Online Application Portal to submit your application and supporting documents.

What You'll Need

  • Notarized CDR Registration Card — A notarized copy of your current, active CDR registration card or official exam results.
  • Official College Transcripts — Showing a master's or doctoral degree from an ACEND-accredited program.
  • Supervised Practice Documentation — Verification of at least 900 hours of supervised pre-professional practice.
  • Criminal Background Check — Completed background check ($32 fee paid as part of the application).
  • Fingerprint background check — through IdentoGO / BCA
  • Application fee — $282

How to Apply

  1. Step 1

    Complete Online Application

    Log in to the Minnesota Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Online Application Portal and complete the online application for either a Licensed Dietitian (LD) or Licensed Nutritionist (LN) license.

  2. Step 2

    Submit Required Documentation

    Arrange for official transcripts, verification of supervised practice, and verification of passing the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination to be sent directly to the Board.

  3. Step 3

    Complete Criminal Background Check

    Follow the specific instructions provided within the Minnesota Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Online Application Portal for completing the required criminal background check.

  4. Step 4

    Pay Application Fee

    Submit the non-refundable initial application fee of $100 through the Minnesota Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Online Application Portal.

  5. Step 5

    Board Review and Notification

    The Minnesota Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice will review your complete application and supporting documents at their regularly scheduled meetings and notify you of your licensure status.

What Usually Slows Applicants Down

  • Fingerprinting delays — Out-of-state prints can add 2-4 weeks to your timeline.
  • Missing or incomplete documents — Have all your documents ready before starting the application to avoid processing delays.
  • Application errors — Double-check that your name matches across all documents exactly. Mismatches are a common reason for delays.

Out-of-State Dietitians

To practice as a dietitian in Minnesota, apply for licensure by reciprocity directly through the Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice. Here's everything you need to get started.

Key Timeline Factors

Keep these requirements in mind, as they are the most likely to impact your processing timeline in Minnesota.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Minnesota can add 2-4 weeks.
  • License verification: Minnesota requires verification of your current out-of-state license before your file is complete.
  • Compact status: No active compact route here. Plan on endorsement.
  • Temporary license: The board does not list a temporary route for this path.

Minnesota Endorsement Requirements

This is the step-by-step endorsement checklist for already-licensed dietitians applying to practice in Minnesota.

  1. Step 1

    Submit Your Application

    Apply through the Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice. The application fee is $100.

  2. Step 2

    Provide License Verification

    Minnesota requires official verification of your active out-of-state license. This must be sent directly to the board from your current state board or its official online verification system before your file can be considered complete.

  3. Step 3

    Confirm Your Notarized CDR Registration Card

    A notarized copy of your current, active CDR registration card or official exam results.

  4. Step 4

    Request Official College Transcripts

    Showing a master's or doctoral degree from an ACEND-accredited program.

  5. Step 5

    Provide Supervised Practice Documentation

    Verification of at least 900 hours of supervised pre-professional practice.

  6. Step 6

    Provide Criminal Background Check

    Completed background check ($32 fee paid as part of the application).

  7. Step 7

    Complete Fingerprinting and Background Screening

    Minnesota requires a criminal background check for all applicants. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Minnesota can add 2-4 weeks to your processing time.

  8. Step 8

    Await Board Processing

    Plan for a processing timeline of about 2 to 4 Weeks once your file is complete.

  9. Step 9

    Board Review and Notification

    The Minnesota Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice will review your complete application and supporting documents at their regularly scheduled meetings and notify you of your licensure status.

Fees, Processing Time, and Renewal

Here is a complete breakdown of the costs and timelines associated with getting and maintaining your Minnesota Dietitian license.

Initial Licensing Costs

Application fee
$100
Background check
$32
Total estimated cost
$282 (Covers your application fee plus the initial license issuance fee)

Processing Timeline

Estimated time
2 to 4 Weeks (once your application and fingerprinting are fully received by the board)

Minnesota Dietitian License Renewal

Maintain your active licensed dietitian and stay compliant with the Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice.

Ongoing License Renewal

Renewal cycle
Annual (by Nov 30) + 45 CE hours per 3 years
Renewal fee
$75

Sources verified February 25, 2026. Links go directly to official Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice pages.

Minnesota Dietitian Licensing FAQ

How much does a Minnesota dietitian license cost?

The total initial cost is $282: $100 application fee, $150 initial license fee, and $32 criminal background check fee. Annual renewal is $75, due by November 30 each year.

What are the continuing education requirements for Minnesota dietitians?

Minnesota requires 45 continuing education units (CEUs) every three years for state license renewal. Additionally, CDR registration requires 75 CPEUs every five years — many activities satisfy both requirements.

Does Minnesota require a notarized CDR card?

Yes. Minnesota specifically requires a notarized copy of your current CDR registration card or official examination results as part of the license application. This is an unusual requirement among states.

Does Minnesota have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Minnesota. To work in Minnesota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Minnesota?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

Trending States

  • Iowa
  • North Dakota
  • South Dakota
  • Wisconsin
